events.name: Fujairah tanker sabotage
events.note: Four tankers sabotaged off Fujairah, UAE.

events.name: Gulf of Oman tanker attacks
events.note: Front Altair and Kokuka Courageous attacked near the Strait.

events.name: Stena Impero seizure
events.note: Iran's IRGC seized the British-flagged tanker Stena Impero.

events.name: Abqaiq-Khurais drone/missile attack
events.note: Attack on Saudi Aramco facilities knocked out ~5% of global oil supply. Not inside the Strait itself, but the largest single Gulf-region shock in this era — useful as a volatility benchmark.

events.name: Soleimani strike & Iranian retaliation
events.note: US strike killed Qasem Soleimani; Iran retaliated with missile strikes on US bases in Iraq on Jan 8, 2020.

events.name: Houthi Red Sea shipping attacks escalation
events.note: US/UK strikes on Houthi targets in Yemen after weeks of Red Sea shipping attacks. Geographically this is Bab-el-Mandeb, not the Strait of Hormuz — included per the project brief, but its result should be read as a distinct chokepoint event, not a Hormuz disruption.

methodology: For each event, the HRI is rebuilt from historical GDELT + EIA data in a [-30, +14] day window. 'hri_rose_at_event' checks whether the HRI at the event date exceeds its own pre-event 30-day mean. This is a sanity check on index responsiveness, not a predictive-power claim — checking whether the HRI would have flagged elevated risk BEFORE each event is a natural next step once more granular intraday data is available.
